item.title: Complicated Acute Appendicitis Conservatively Treated in Two Patients With Crohn's Disease: The Role of Intestinal Ultrasound and Contrast-Enhanced Ultrasound.
item.description: Journal of clinical ultrasound : JCU - Case report - According to the most recent guidelines, uncomplicated acute appendicitis could be treated conservatively using broad-spectrum antibiotics, but if radiological signs of appendix rupture are present...

item.title: Microcystic lymphatic malformation of the small intestine presenting as acute abdominal pain in a 5-year-old boy: a case report and review of the literature.
item.description: Journal of medical case reports - Lymphatic malformations are rare benign congenital anomalies of the lymphatic system, most commonly affecting the head and neck. Gastrointestinal involvement is uncommon, and lymphatic malformation...
